title: iam-jit dynamic-deny rules file
description:
Schema for ~/.iam-jit/dynamic-denies.yaml — the operator-managed pool of in-flight deny rules that fan out across every Bounce-suite product (ibounce, kbounce, dbounce, gbounce). Authored interactively via `iam-jit deny add` or conversationally via the `bounce_deny_add` MCP tool. Watched by every running bouncer for hot reload (inotify on Linux; fsevents on macOS). Per the post-`[[discovery-first-default]]` pivot this is the primary opt-in deny ergonomics surface; the recommender (per #324f) ALSO consumes this file so that JIT-issued roles embed the same denies as explicit `Deny` statements (defense-in-depth: bouncer denies at request time + role embeds the constraint).

File-on-disk requirements (enforced by the writer; verified by the reader):
  * Path: `~/.iam-jit/dynamic-denies.yaml` (override via `IAM_JIT_DYNAMIC_DENIES_PATH`).
  * Permissions: 0600 (read/write owner only).
  * Format: YAML 1.2 (round-trippable; preserves operator comments).
  * Watcher: bouncer processes subscribe to fsevents/inotify; corruption falls back to the LAST-KNOWN-GOOD in-memory snapshot + emits an admin-action OCSF event with `kind=dynamic_deny.file_corrupt` + `severity=high`.

The authoritative builder ships in #324e (`src/iam_jit/cli_deny.py` + `src/iam_jit/dynamic_denies/store.py`); the per-bouncer consumers ship in #324a-d.

schema_version.description: Wire-format version. Matches the cross-product common-subset convention from `schemas/INDEX.md` (string, not int).

product.description: Magic discriminator so a misrouted `ibounce-config.yaml` (or vice versa) gets refused at parse rather than silently mis-applied.

exported_at.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp the file was last written. Updated by every `iam-jit deny add/remove` invocation.

source_hostname_hash.description: sha256[:12] of the host that authored the file. Privacy-preserving provenance per `[[cross-product-agent-parity]]`.

denies.description: Ordered list of active deny rules. Order is preserved on disk (newest-last) but evaluation is by full match — order does not affect precedence per the design doc's `Conflict resolution` section.

id.description: ULID-suffixed unique identifier (`dd_<ULID>`). ULIDs are 26 chars of Crockford base32 (rejects I/L/O/U to avoid digit confusion). Stable across the lifetime of the rule; referenced by `iam-jit deny remove ID` + the `bounce_deny_remove(id)` MCP tool.

targets.description: One or more target patterns. Cross-protocol — the resolver in `iam-jit deny add` classifies each pattern by shape (ARN → ibounce; hostname/RDS endpoint → dbounce + gbounce; k8s namespace/cluster → kbouncer; URL/glob → gbounce) and writes the destination(s) into `applied_to`. See the design doc's `Cross-protocol target resolver table` for exact heuristics + ambiguous-pattern handling.

reason.description: Operator-supplied free-text reason. Surfaces verbatim in the bouncer's `403`-payload `deny_reason` field + the OCSF audit event so the next operator sees `why` without context-switching.

duration.description: Either a Go-style duration string (`30m`, `3h`, `7d`) OR the literal `permanent`. The duration anchors `expires_at`; permanent denies have `expires_at: null`.

added_by.description: Audit-trail metadata. Resolved via `resolve_operator()` — typically `email@org.com` (OIDC) or `username@hostname` (local). Mirrors `admin-action-event.schema.json::admin_action.actor`.

added_at.description: ISO 8601 / RFC3339 timestamp the rule was created. Source of truth for `expires_at = added_at + duration` (computed at write-time, not read-time, so clock drift on the read host does not extend the deny window).

expires_at.description: When the rule auto-removes. `null` for `duration: permanent`. The watcher in each bouncer schedules removal at this timestamp + emits a `dynamic_deny.expired` admin-action event when fired.

applied_to.description: Which bouncer(s) the deny is meant for, resolved at `deny add` time from the targets. A target pattern that resolves to multiple bouncers (e.g. an RDS hostname matches both dbounce + gbounce) yields multiple entries. An empty resolution is rejected at add-time with a structured error.

applies_to_recommender.description: Per #324f: when true (default), the iam-jit recommender embeds an explicit `Deny` statement matching the targets into any role it issues for the lifetime of the rule. Setting false scopes the rule to the bouncer-only enforcement path (useful when the deny target is non-AWS or the operator explicitly wants bouncer-only enforcement).

source.description: Provenance — how the rule landed. `org-distributed` rules cannot be loosened by personal denies per the design doc's `Conflict resolution` section. `threat-feed` rules are auto-applied by the threat-feed applier (#407-#411 / #645).

org_distributed_url.description: When `source == "org-distributed"`: the HTTPS URL the rule was installed from. Used at sync time to refresh.
